kc: Identify why the useState hook is necessary
question: Why is the useState hook necessary in a React component?

options.A: It allows React to track values and re-render the component when those values change
options.B: It lets JavaScript variables be used inside JSX
options.C: It automatically updates all HTML elements in the component
options.D: It is required whenever a component uses a button

feedback.A: Correct! useState allows React to track state values and re-render the component whenever those values change.
feedback.B: useState is not required just to display values in JSX. Regular variables can also be displayed, but useState is needed when the value changes and the UI should update.
feedback.C: useState does not directly update HTML elements. Instead, it tells React that state has changed so React can re-render the component.
feedback.D: Buttons do not require useState. useState is only needed when a component must track changing values and update the UI.

kc: Identify why the useState hook is necessary
question: What problem does useState solve in a React component?

options.A: It allows a component to remember changing values between renders
options.B: It allows functions to return JSX
options.D: It allows components to call other components

feedback.A: Correct! useState allows a component to preserve values between renders and update the UI when those values change.
feedback.B: Components can return JSX without useState. The purpose of useState is to manage changing values that should persist between renders.
feedback.C: useState is not used to make variables update faster. It is used so React can track state changes and re-render the component when needed.
feedback.D: Components can render other components without useState. The purpose of useState is to store values that can change over time.
